source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A spherical pendulum consists of a point mass $m$ tied by a string of length $l$ to a fixed point, so that it is constrained to move on a spherical surface as shown in Fig. 2.14. The mass in the circular orbit as in part (a) above receives an impulse perpendicular to its velocity, resulting in an orbit which has its highest point with the string making an angle $\theta_{1}$ with the vertical. Write down (but do not try to solve) the equation which may be solved for the angle the string makes with the vertical when the mass is at its lowest point.

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A spherical pendulum consists of a point mass $m$ tied by a string of length $l$ to a fixed point, so that it is constrained to move on a spherical surface as shown in Fig. 2.14. The mass in the circular orbit as in part (a) above receives an impulse perpendicular to its velocity, resulting in an orbit which has its highest point with the string making an angle $\theta_{1}$ with the vertical. Write down (but do not try to solve) the equation which may be solved for the angle the string makes with the vertical when the mass is at its lowest point.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL object representation rather than a concrete file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain:
At the highest point of the orbit of $m$, $\dot{	heta}=0$ and $	heta=	heta_{1}$, giving
$$K=\omega^{2} rac{\sin^{4}	heta_{0}}{\sin^{2}	heta_{1}}-2\omega_{0}^{2}\cos	heta_{0}\cos	heta_{1}.$$ 
At the lowest point, $\dot{	heta}=0$, $	heta=	heta_{2}$, and we have
$$ rac{\sin^{4}	heta_{0}}{\cos	heta_{0}}\Big( rac{1}{\sin^{2}	heta_{1}}- rac{1}{\sin^{2}	heta_{2}}\Big)+2(\cos	heta_{2}-\cos	heta_{1})=0,$$
which may be solved for $	heta_{2}$ in terms of $	heta_{0}$ and $	heta_{1}$.

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain:
At the highest point of the orbit of $m$, $\dot{	heta}=0$ and $	heta=	heta_{1}$, giving
$$K=\omega^{2} rac{\sin^{4}	heta_{0}}{\sin^{2}	heta_{1}}-2\omega_{0}^{2}\cos	heta_{0}\cos	heta_{1}.$$ 
At the lowest point, $\dot{	heta}=0$, $	heta=	heta_{2}$, and we have
$$ rac{\sin^{4}	heta_{0}}{\cos	heta_{0}}\Big( rac{1}{\sin^{2}	heta_{1}}- rac{1}{\sin^{2}	heta_{2}}\Big)+2(\cos	heta_{2}-\cos	heta_{1})=0,$$
